citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'behavioral-eval AC: fixture created at docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/citation-grounding-coverage.md (two-plan scenario, N=10/>=80% threshold); live LLM eval is operator-run and deferred per the fixture — no in-session LLM eval was executed.'

findings.criterion: RUBRIC BEHAVIORAL EVAL (runtime, not text-presence): a plan-review behavioral eval fixture under `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/` exercises the Layer-2 coverage protocol on TWO plans … run over N samples against a predeclared threshold … PROVE: the eval harness run recorded on the ticket. [operator-attested]
findings.detail: The operator-attested criterion requires proof of an actual eval harness run — a recorded pass-rate vs. threshold for both plans. The ticket comment explicitly states 'no in-session LLM eval was executed' and describes the live eval as 'operator-run and deferred.' The attestation names only the fixture file created (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/citation-grounding-coverage.md) but contains no measured outcome (sample pass-rate, run timestamp, or threshold clearance). An attestation of a deferred, unrun eval does not satisfy the 'harness run recorded on the ticket' requirement; no specific pass-rate or run evidence is provided.
findings.remediation: Run the behavioral eval harness against the two-plan fixture at docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/citation-grounding-coverage.md (N=10 per plan, >=80% threshold), record the per-plan pass-rates (e.g. 'Plan A: 9/10, Plan B: 8/10') and the run date/model version as a ticket comment or linked artifact.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
